---
title: "Raster-enabling Apache Hop"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-04-03 11:42"
image: "building-heights.png"
description: "Stefan Ziegler continues his series on #geoenabling #ApacheHop, this time adding #raster support to his hop-gdal-plugin. Stefan walks through an #ETL pipeline that computes building heights from LiDAR and vector data using new raster transforms (Raster Clip, Raster Zonal Stats) built on the newer #GDAL tool structure."
---

Stefan Ziegler's efforts to geo-enable Apache Hop[^hop]
([previously][geo-enabling]) continues: After vector data support through OGR[^ogr]
reader and writer plug-ins, a geometry inspector, some INTERLIS[^interlis] integration,
and more, Stefan is now tackling raster data. In
[*Let's Hop 6: Pixel für Pixel*][hop6] (alternatively: [on LinkedIn][linkedin]),
he describes^[in German] how he added several raster transforms to his
[`hop-gdal-plugin`][hop-plugin] using the newer GDAL tool structure.[^gdal-tools]

To demonstrate the new capabilities, Stefan walks through a pipeline that
computes building heights from LiDAR[^lidar] and vector data: an OGR reader ingests
building footprints from a GeoPackage[^geopackage] file, while a *Raster Clip* transform
clips the corresponding extent from an externally hosted COG[^cog] file. The two
streams are joined[^hop-unwieldy], and a
*Raster Zonal Stats* transform[^hop-gui] is used to calculate average and maximum height per building.
The result is written back to GeoPackage via an *OGR output*.

![Demo workflow (source: Stefan Ziegler)](workflow.png "Demo workflow (source: Stefan Ziegler)")

Nice to see the geospatial capabilities of Hop further expanded. To recap
Stefan's series[^stefan-blog-links]:

- [Let's Hop 1: Laying the Foundation][hop1]^[While some articles have English 
titles, they are all written in German.]
- [Let's Hop 2: Die Idee ist gut, die Vorschau ist besser][hop2]
- [Let's Hop 3: A native ili2db Integration for Apache Hop][hop3]
- [Let's Hop 4: Ich möchte Teil einer Validierung sein][hop4]
- [Let's Hop 5: Geometrien bearbeiten, Layer vergleichen, Coverages prüfen][hop5]
- [Let's Hop 6: Pixel für Pixel][hop6]

Stefan's *"[INTERLIS made easy](https://blog.sogeo.services/archive.html)"*
series is at 61 instalments. Let's see how far *"Let's Hop"* will go.[^fingers-crossed] 

[^hop]: If you need a reminder: [Apache Hop](https://hop.apache.org/) is an
open-source ETL (extract-transform-load) solution that – in its own words and
certainly in the hopes of some stakeholders – "aims to be the future of data
integration."
[^ogr]: OGR is the vector data library within [GDAL](https://gdal.org/) supporting a wide range of vector formats.
[^interlis]: [INTERLIS](https://www.interlis.ch/en) is a Swiss standard for geodata modelling and data exchange.
[^gdal-tools]: Think, for example, `gdal raster hillshade` instead of the very
broad `gdal_translate` or `gdalwarp`.
[^lidar]: Light Detection and Ranging, an active remote sensing technique that uses laser pulses to generate 3D point clouds of the Earth's surface or objects.
[^geopackage]: An open SQLite-based container format for geospatial data, defined by the [OGC](https://www.ogc.org/).
[^cog]: Cloud-optimized GeoTIFF.
[^hop-unwieldy]: Here, Hop feels somewhat unwieldy, I would say.
[^hop-gui]: Here, Stefan finds the user interface somewhat overwhelming.
[^stefan-blog-links]: The articles are all on LinkedIn, as well, but here I use the more sovereign links to Stefan's blog.
[^fingers-crossed]: 🤞– which, of course, is far from a sustainable strategy.

[geo-enabling]: https://spatialists.ch/posts/2026/03/10-geo-enabling-apache-hop/
[linkedin]: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/lets-hop-6-pixel-f%C3%BCr-stefan-ziegler-0me3e
[hop1]: https://blog.sogeo.services/blog/2026/03/06/lets-hop-01.html
[hop2]: https://blog.sogeo.services/blog/2026/03/09/lets-hop-02.html
[hop3]: https://blog.sogeo.services/blog/2026/03/13/lets-hop-03.html
[hop4]: https://blog.sogeo.services/blog/2026/03/16/lets-hop-04.html
[hop5]: https://blog.sogeo.services/blog/2026/03/17/lets-hop-05.html
[hop6]: https://blog.sogeo.services/blog/2026/03/30/lets-hop-06.html
[hop-plugin]: https://github.com/edigonzales/hop-distributions
